MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jun-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Nicholas W. Heldt, Esq. & Gayle L. Gough, Esq. of Sedwick Detert, (415) 781-7900, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; William K. Sanders, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-6771, appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary adjudication is granted in part, and denied in part. Motion is denied as to No. 1, that Electric service to Ferry Building constitutes a "new account" under the Master Settlement Agreement. Motion is granted as to the remaining issues: 2) There has been a material change in the use and activity at the Ferry Building following the remodeling project; 3) Electric service to the Ferry Building Investors at the Ferry Building does not qualify as municipal load under the 1987 Interconnection Agreement. 4)PG&E is not required by the 1987 Interconnection Agreement to transport Hetch Hetchy power to Ferry Building Investors or subtenants at the Ferry Building; 5) CCSF is prohibited by the 1987 Interconnection Agreement from selling PG&E transmitted electricity to Ferry Building Investors, or subtenants at the Ferry Building; and, 6) PG&E is entitled to serve electricity to Ferry Building Investors and its subtenants at the Ferry Building. Court also bases is ruling on: Stipulated Facts and Stipulated documents; and, 1) Paragraph 1.43 1 A; 2) 9.1 of the Rents Act; 3) CCSF in violation of 1.43; 4) Ferry Building use; 5, CCSF is in violation of Sec. 6. of the Raker Act. The Court will accept one Order for both motions, Counsel to prepare Order. (Two Orders re objections to Evid. filed by CCSF, and, Two Orders re: objections to Evid. filed by Plft. PG&E. Copies given to counsel at hearing.) (302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Nicholas W. Heldt, Esq. & Gayle L. Gough, Esq. of Sedwick Detert, (415) 781-7900, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; William K. Sanders, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-6771, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Court also bases is ruling on Stipulated Facts and Stipulated documents; and, 1) Paragraph 1.43 1 A; 2) 9.1 of the Rents Act; 3) CCSF in violation of 1.43; 4) Ferry Building use; 5, CCSF is in violation of Sec. 6. of the Rakor Act. The Court will accept one Order for both motions on today's calendar in this matter, Counsel to prepare Order. (302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
